title: quercus coccifera var. rigida
intro: Quercus coccifera var. rigida, also known as the Kermes oak, is a deciduous shrub or small tree belonging to the Quercus genus, native to the Mediterranean region. The bark of this plant contains a high concentration of kermes, a resinous secretion that has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, particularly as a natural dye and astringent. The health benefits of Quercus coccifera var. rigida include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable natural remedy for various skin conditions. Traditionally, the kermes oak has been used in herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are used to treat skin issues like eczema, acne, and dermatitis, as well as to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
